title: Kalshi's Crypto Perpetuals Spark Debate Over Whether They're Futures or Swaps
summary: A clash between derivatives veterans is exposing a deeper discussion over how U.S. regulators should classify crypto perpetual contracts, with Kalshi's new product at the center of the debate.
body:
Kalshi, a U.S.-based prediction market platform, has launched crypto perpetual contracts, sparking a heated debate among derivatives experts and regulators. These contracts, which allow traders to bet on the future price of cryptocurrencies without an expiration date, have raised questions about their proper classification under U.S. regulations.

The debate centers on whether these perpetual contracts should be treated as futures or swaps. Futures are standardized contracts traded on exchanges, while swaps are customized agreements between two parties. The distinction is crucial because it determines which regulatory framework applies and how these products can be offered to investors. A clash between derivatives veterans is exposing a deeper discussion over how U.S. regulators should classify crypto perpetual contracts.

This regulatory uncertainty affects everyday crypto traders who may be unsure about the legal status and protections of these products. Clearer regulations could provide more transparency and security for investors, helping them make informed decisions about trading crypto derivatives.

As the debate continues, traders and regulators alike are watching closely to see how the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) will address this issue. The outcome could set a precedent for how other crypto derivatives are classified and regulated in the future. For now, traders should stay informed about any regulatory updates that may impact their trading strategies.
twitterThread: Kalshi's new crypto perpetual contracts have experts debating whether they're futures or swaps. The classification could reshape crypto derivatives regulation.
The debate hinges on whether these contracts should be treated as standardized futures or customized swaps. The CFTC's decision will have major implications for traders.

If you trade crypto derivatives, keep an eye on how this plays out. The outcome could change how these products are regulated and offered. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-13-kalshis-crypto-perpetuals-spark-regulatory-debate-over-classification
